title: On macOS, mounting the current directory with "." fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On macOS, mounting the current directory with "." fails. This causes this podman-compose mount directive to fail:

```
# ...
    volumes:
      - type: bind
        source: .
        target: /a
# ...
```

However, binding with "$(pwd)" works.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
podman machine init -v $HOME:$HOME
podman machine start
mkdir a
cd a
touch b
podman run -v .:/a alpine ls a  
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:213ec9aee27d8be045c6a92b7eac22c9a64b44558193775a1a7f626352392b49
Copying config sha256:9c6f0724472873bb50a2ae67a9e7adcb57673a183cea8b06eb778dca859181b5
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ls: can't open 'a': Permission denied
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
b
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Running `podman run -v $(pwd):/a alpine ls a` results in the expected output.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out.  I can reproduce the issue on Linux as well.

Docker throws the following error:
> docker: Error response from daemon: create .: volume name is too short, names should be at least two alphanumeric characters.

Docker doesn't support this at all (no ability to do relative paths) - so it's trying to make a named volume.

Podman is actually trying to treat the path as relative, but it looks like that's going very poorly with the remote client. Probably need to turn the relative path into an absolute path early on if the remote client is in use.

Can we not let clients always supply an absolute path? If docker-compose calls docker always with an absolute path when binding volumes, I think podman-compose should do the same
________________________________
From: Matt Heon ***@***.***>
Sent: Monday, November 14, 2022 9:24:20 AM
To: containers/podman ***@***.***>
Cc: Mike Veldink ***@***.***>; Author ***@***.***>
Subject: Re: [containers/podman] On macOS, mounting the current directory with "." fails (Issue #16455)


Docker doesn't support this at all (no ability to do relative paths) - so it's trying to make a named volume.

Podman is actually trying to treat the path as relative, but it looks like that's going very poorly with the remote client. Probably need to turn the relative path into an absolute path early on if the remote client is in use.

—
Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub<https://nam12.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fcontainers%2Fpodman%2Fissues%2F16455%23issuecomment-1313808031&data=05%7C01%7Cmveldink%40mediamath.com%7C33ee4f01ba1e448ec17908dac64bebfd%7C85d8894b475e4dd5b6364d92f529f7aa%7C1%7C0%7C638040326668843891%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=90X04Oa%2Ft5qu1%2Bf%2FOAAif2AvVkLMF%2BZ1SSoSpAfA8Zk%3D&reserved=0>, or unsubscribe<https://nam12.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fnotifications%2Funsubscribe-auth%2FAXHUI7UZCJZLMMGE6OAG34LWIJDRJANCNFSM6AAAAAAR3MIRRU&data=05%7C01%7Cmveldink%40mediamath.com%7C33ee4f01ba1e448ec17908dac64bebfd%7C85d8894b475e4dd5b6364d92f529f7aa%7C1%7C0%7C638040326668843891%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=noTn4S5X5Z9IaqTZA4WwrBwna%2BOI4KMUfDEIDLHzCEA%3D&reserved=0>.
You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID: ***@***.***>
Docker-compose forces absolute paths because Docker requires absolute paths to function; Podman does work with relative paths, so I don't think podman-compose should have to care.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is not the podman-compose project, I am closing.
